content.problemStatement:
--

**Now, Let's make the Nutrition analyzer a little more interesting using inheritance**

- Use "Food" class as the base class for the different food items.

- Create two sub-classes, "Fruit" and "FastFood", that extend the "Food" class and add their respective additional properties:
   - For the "Fruit" sub-class, add the "vitaminContent" property with an appropriate data type (e.g., a String for the vitamin type).
   - For the "FastFood" sub-class, add the "fatPercentage" property with an appropriate data type (e.g., a double).

- Override the "display()" method in both sub-classes to include the newly added properties:
   - For the Fruit sub-class, display the name, calories, and vitamin content.
   - For the FastFood sub-class, display the name, calories, and fat percentage.

- Inside the "main" method, instantiate objects of both "Fruit" and "FastFood" sub-classes, like so:

   - A fruit object with name, calories, and vitamin content specified.
   - A fast food object with name, calories, and fat percentage specified.

- For each instantiated object, call the "display()" method to demonstrate inheritance and showcase the nutrition information of the food item.

--
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
